title: Chocolate cake – rich, moist and terribly delicious
excerpt: I spent 3 days trying to develop the best chocolate cake there is. It had to be something between a cake, a mousse and a brownie. Here it is.

seo.title: Chocolate cake - rich, moist and terribly delicious - Marinda Kook

html: I spent 3 days trying to develop the best chocolate cake there is. It had to be something between a cake, a mousse and a brownie. Here it is.

recipe.title: Chocolate cake – rich, moist and terribly delicious
recipe.author: Marinda Engelbrecht

image.alt: Sjokoladekoek - ryk, nat en verskriklik lekker

items: 250 ml boiling water with 10 ml coffee powder dissolved in it

steps: Use two regular cake pans and line the bottoms with baking paper and spray the pans with Spray and Cook.
Sift the flour, cocoa, baking powder, baking soda and salt together in a mixing bowl.
Whisk in the sugar, then the eggs, then the milk, then the oil and lastly the hot water with the coffee. The batter looks thin, but that's right.
Divide the batter between your cake pans and bake for 30 – 35 minutes. Test with a skewer to see if it's done.  Don't overbake.
Let it cool in the pans for 10 minutes and then turn it out onto a wire rack.

directionGroups.title: Icing
steps: Whisk the cocoa powder into the melted butter.
Alternately whisk in the icing sugar and milk. Check if the consistency looks right, otherwise add more milk.

notes: I put caramel and icing sugar in the middle. Just icing sugar works just as well.
